title: DDL, Constraints, Schema Evolution, and Integrity Repair · Query Trace: Integrity Constraints
summary: The support schema must reject impossible states and evolve while old application versions are still active. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql integrity constraints increment in a constraint-rich, reversible schema migration. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enforce NOT NULL, UNIQUE, CHECK, primary-key, and foreign-key invariants at the database boundary.
Define tables with deliberate types, defaults, generated values, and named constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Build Integrity Constraints evidence · Theory Integrity Constraints
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-integrity-constraints-v807n2" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; DDL constraint, migration, or integrity-repair evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Integrity Constraints evidence · Theory Integrity Constraints.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Integrity Constraints into observable performance evidence. For Build Integrity Constraints evidence · Theory Integrity Constraints, keep Integrity Constraints connected to executable relational evidence.

-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
CREATE TABLE lab_assignments_v807n2 (
  ticket_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES tickets(ticket_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  assigned_at TEXT NOT NULL
);
